lessons.title: At the Bus Stop
lessons.scene: You're at a crowded BMTC bus stop in Majestic. You need to reach Whitefield but aren't sure which bus to take. An older man waits beside you.

steps.prompt: Ask the man which bus goes to Whitefield

choices.hint: 'Eshtu' (how much) doesn't fit here. To ask 'which bus', use 'yaava bus'.

correctReaction.speaker: Old Man
correctReaction.kannada: Three thirty five bus hogutte. Alli nilteyalli nilli.
correctReaction.script: ಥ್ರೀ ಥರ್ಟಿ ಫೈವ್ ಬಸ್ ಹೋಗುತ್ತೆ. ಅಲ್ಲಿ ನಿಲ್ದಾಣದಲ್ಲಿ ನಿಲ್ಲಿ.
correctReaction.pronunciation: three-thirty-five bus ho-GUT-te. AL-li nil-DAA-na-dal-li NIL-li
correctReaction.english: The 335 bus goes there. Wait at that stop over there.

wrongReaction.speaker: Old Man
wrongReaction.english: Tell me which place you want.

steps.grammarTip: Question words: 'yaava' = which, 'yaaru' = who, 'enu' = what, 'elli' = where, 'eshtu' = how much/many. Pick the right one — using 'eshtu' (how much) when you mean 'yaava' (which) is a common beginner slip.

steps.speaker: Conductor
steps.english: Tickets, tickets! Who hasn't taken a ticket?

steps.prompt: Ask for one ticket to Whitefield

choices.hint: Riding without a ticket gets you fined! Ask for one: 'ondu ticket kodi'.
That's his question to ask, not yours. Just say how many: 'ondu ticket' (one ticket).

correctReaction.speaker: Conductor
correctReaction.english: Forty rupees. Please give change, sir.

wrongReaction.speaker: Conductor
wrongReaction.english: If there's no ticket I'll fine you, sir!

steps.grammarTip: To say how many people, put the number before 'jana': 'ondu jana' / 'eradu jana' (one / two people). For tickets you can simply say the number + 'ticket': 'eradu ticket' = two tickets.

lessons.title: At the Railway Station
lessons.scene: You're at Bengaluru City (Majestic) railway station to travel to Mysuru. You walk up to the ticket counter.

steps.prompt: Ask for two tickets to Mysuru

choices.hint: 'Gante' is o'clock, not ticket! Say 'eradu ticket' and the destination with '-ge'.
'-beda' makes it a 'don't'! For tickets, ask: 'eradu ticket kodi'.

correctReaction.speaker: Clerk
correctReaction.kannada: Munoor ippattu rupayi. Train nalku gante-ge.
correctReaction.script: ಮುನ್ನೂರ ಇಪ್ಪತ್ತು ರೂಪಾಯಿ. ಟ್ರೈನ್ ನಾಲ್ಕು ಗಂಟೆಗೆ.
correctReaction.pronunciation: mun-NOO-ra IP-pat-tu ru-PAA-yi. train NAAL-ku GAN-te-ge
correctReaction.english: Three hundred twenty rupees. The train is at four o'clock.

wrongReaction.speaker: Clerk
wrongReaction.kannada: Eshtu ticket beku saar, sariyaagi heli.
wrongReaction.script: ಎಷ್ಟು ಟಿಕೆಟ್ ಬೇಕು ಸಾರ್, ಸರಿಯಾಗಿ ಹೇಳಿ.
wrongReaction.pronunciation: ESH-tu TI-ket BEH-ku saar, sa-ri-YAA-gi HEH-li
wrongReaction.english: How many tickets do you need sir, tell me properly.

steps.grammarTip: '-ge' on a place means 'to': 'Mysuru-ge' = to Mysuru, 'elli-ge' = to where. Note how some place names change shape for '-ge': Mysuru → Mysuru-ge / Mysuri-ge — both are heard.

steps.prompt: Ask which platform the train comes to

choices.english: Which platform does the train come to?
How many platforms does the train have?
choices.hint: You want WHICH platform, not how many. Use 'yaava platform-ge barutte?'.
'Yaavaga' = when (about time). For which platform, use 'yaava platform'.

correctReaction.speaker: Clerk
correctReaction.kannada: Mooru-ne platform saar. Muncheye hogi nilli.
correctReaction.script: ಮೂರನೇ ಪ್ಲಾಟ್‌ಫಾರ್ಮ್ ಸಾರ್. ಮುಂಚೆಯೇ ಹೋಗಿ ನಿಲ್ಲಿ.
correctReaction.pronunciation: MOO-ru-ne PLAAT-faarm saar. MUN-che-ye HO-gi NIL-li
correctReaction.english: Platform number three, sir. Go and wait there early.

steps.grammarTip: Add '-ne' to a number for the ordinal: 'mooru' (three) → 'mooru-ne' (third), 'ondu' → 'ondane' (first). 'Yaavaga' (when) vs 'yaava' (which) sound alike — 'yaavaga' has the extra 'ga' for time.
